name: descriptive-action
description: Use when the user asks to describe, summarize, analyze, compare, explain, or report on something (text, data, events, systems) without asking for recommendations or next steps.
capabilities: Produce neutral descriptions of text, data, events, or systems
Analyze and compare information with clear structure
Extract and report facts with evidence references
Summarize complex topics in organized sections
Identify trends, constraints, and open questions
author: BellaBe
securitySummary: All 34 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill containing only behavioral guidelines. The static scanner incorrectly flagged metadata hash strings (content_hash, tree_hash), source repository URLs, and English words like 'hash', 'system', and 'compare' as security threats. No executable code, network calls, filesystem operations, or external commands exist. The skill-report.json already correctly classified this as risk_level: safe.
valueStatement: Users often need accurate, neutral descriptions without recommendations or next steps. This skill delivers structured analysis focusing on facts and observations. It helps produce clear, factual descriptions of text, data, events, or systems.
limitations: Does not provide recommendations or prescribe actions
Does not create plans or strategies
Cannot answer what should I do type questions
Limited to descriptive analysis within stated scope

useCases.title: Analyze Study Findings
useCases.description: Summarize research data and extract key facts from complex documents

useCases.title: Explain Code Systems
useCases.description: Describe code architecture and compare implementation approaches objectively

useCases.title: Document Features
useCases.description: Report system status and document technical concepts with structured format

promptTemplates.title: Simple Description
promptTemplates.prompt: Describe [topic] focusing on key characteristics and how it works.

promptTemplates.title: Comparative Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze and compare [option A] versus [option B] on key criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide a structured summary of the following data including background, findings, and trends.

promptTemplates.title: Fact Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the main facts and evidence from this document and identify any knowledge gaps.

bestPractices: State scope and constraints upfront to focus the analysis
Request evidence and sources to support factual claims
Separate verified facts from reasonable inferences clearly
antiPatterns: Asking for recommendations when you need only facts
Using vague prompts without clear subject or scope
Mixing descriptive requests with prescriptive questions

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code platforms.

faq.question: What are the input size limits?
faq.answer: The skill handles text, data, and artifacts within standard AI context limits.

faq.question: How does it integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Use it after data collection skills to analyze results without prescribing actions.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only prompt guidelines and makes no external calls.

faq.question: Why is output structured?
faq.answer: Structured format ensures clarity, consistency, and easy parsing of descriptive content.

faq.question: How is this different from prescriptive skills?
faq.answer: Descriptive skills analyze and report facts. Prescriptive skills recommend actions.
